rationale: Both candidates independently converge on the same substantive design point ground truth reviewer dsocek raised (hardcoded default_components duplicates per-pipeline metadata; derive from a single source of truth), giving comparable recall. X is better calibrated: it recommends COMMENT/approve-level, matching the actual outcome (two LGTM approvals), and adds a plausible, well-grounded extra finding (text-encoder LoRA silent no-op) with clean, non-redundant output. Y recommends REQUEST CHANGES — a mismatch with the real merge outcome — and its test-coverage ask (require a real StableDiffusionXLPipeline instantiation) is inconsistent with the codebase's established bare-nn.Module unit-test convention, weakening precision; Y's output is also verbatim tripled, a generation artifact that undercuts trust and clarity.
